sections.title: Starting out
Talking to the High Priest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you plan to do now?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well now that we've sorted out this business with Klenter, we can properly focus on dealing with these plagues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that looking?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Not great. However, we do have one bit of good news in that area, thanks to you.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. How goes things?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Better now that we've sorted out that business with Klenter. Still, we're no closer to dealing with these plagues.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: However, we do have one bit of good news in that area, thanks to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks to me?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Yes. You've shown us that the pox we're all infected with can't be contagious. If it was, you'd be covered in spots by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a very good point. Does this mean you can reopen the city?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It does indeed. The decision to lock things down was made very quickly, so we were forced to trap many of our citizens outside the walls. We'll be able to let them all back in now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's wonderful news!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It is, but there is a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: When the plague first started, Menaphos, our neighbouring city, closed their gates as well. Many of our citizens became trapped there when they did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if there's no risk of them becoming infected, can't they open up again as well?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You'd think so, but they believe that there is still a danger. It seems they're even willing to kill if it helps keep their city isolated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Perhaps I could help?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The offer is appreciated, but I don't think there's anything you can do.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You'd really be willing to help us again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I'm very grateful. To be honest though, I'm not really sure where to start with this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well first up, why don't you tell me what happened with Menaphos? You said something about them being willing to kill?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Indeed. Earlier today, I led a delegation across the bridge between the two cities in the hope of convincing them to end the quarantine. It didn't go well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I have other things to do.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I see. Fair enough.

sections.title: Cut scene 1 - Sophanem and Menaphos meet

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Halt! Don't take another step!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: That is an improper greeting. You address the High Priest of Icthlarin!

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Don't try my patience! I have been ordered to stop anyone attempting to cross this bridge!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We have discovered that we are not contagious. You must let us pass!

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Step back or we will open fire.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You wouldn't dare!

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Archers, take your mark!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You wouldn't?

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Take aim!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Run!

lines.speaker: Coenus
lines.text: Fire!

sections.title: Talking to High Priest after the cutscene
Talking to the High Priest again before asking if there is a way in from below

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again. Have you had any thoughts on how we can get our citizens out of Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a think about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they just started firing arrows at you? They sound like cold-blooded thugs.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It is often the way under Pharoah Kemesis. He leads the Menaphites in a more secular manner than we would like. Respect for the old ways is diminishing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By old ways, do you mean Icthlarin?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The whole Menaphite Pantheon really. Religion is starting to mean less and less in Menaphos.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: There was once a time when we would care for all of Menaphos' dead here. Now, many opt for less traditional burials.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Anyway, I've let myself become distracted. My focus is often on the dead, but right now our main worry is the living.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It's clear that the Pharaoh has no desire to open his city, so we need to find another way of getting our citizens home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how we do that?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well... I don't know. I'm open to any ideas you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I disguised myself as a citizen or a guard?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It would not work. Their quarantine is very strict, just as ours was. Even their own people are not allowed in or out of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do they get food and other supplies in?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The port south of the main city is still open. Traders take their goods to the city gates and exchange them with the guards there. Everything is thoroughly checked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Doesn't sound like we can make anything work there then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any way across the river?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You'd want to swim across? If the current doesn't kill you, the crocodiles surely will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Maybe not then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could fight my way in?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Absolutely not! We here in Sophanem are still Menaphites and those guards are our brethren. Besides, I doubt you'd succeed against so many of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any way into Menaphos from below?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: I don't know. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. So there aren't any tunnels under this city?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Beyond the homes of our dead? I can't imagine so. We are not ones for digging tunnels unnecessarily. We leave that nonsense to the Sect of Scabaras, and they're long gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Sect of Scabaras?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: They were a group dedicated to Scabaras, one of the lesser deities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a lesser deity?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You already know of Icthlarin and the Devourer. They are the son and daughter of Tumeken and Elidinis. Together, they are the four main deities of the Menaphite Pantheon.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: However, the pantheon also has four lesser deities, or avatars as some call them. The Book of Light and Day says that Tumeken once fell into a deep sleep and dreamt for four days and four nights.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: With each night, his dreams took form. Those four forms became the lesser deities of the pantheon, with Scabaras being the last of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And his followers liked to dig tunnels?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: That's right. Scabaras is the god of isolation. In keeping with his teachings, his followers mostly lived underground. As such, they were said to be great architects of the subterranean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened to them?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The Devourer was not the only member of the pantheon to wish ill upon our people. It is said that many years ago, Scabaras and his sect tried to claim these lands as their own.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: They built great tunnels beneath the Elid and used them to attack our settlements. However, Elidinis stood against them. The sect was destroyed, and Scabaras was fittingly driven into isolation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you don't think there's a chance they might have built some tunnels under Sophanem and Menaphos?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Er... I suppose I've never considered it before. I guess it's not impossible.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Maybe you should visit Jex at the Temple of the Lesser Gods in the north east corner of the city. He knows more about the Sect of Scabaras than I do.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: In fact, now that I think about it, I recall a rumour that the temple was actually once home to the sect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. I'll pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to think some more.

sections.title: Talking to High Priest again, before visiting Jex

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again. Did you visit Jex at the Temple of the Lesser Gods?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: You'll find the temple in the north east corner of the city.

sections.title: The Route into Menaphos
Talking to Jex

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Are you Jex?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: That's right. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm hoping to learn a bit more about the Sect of Scabaras. The High Priest said you'd be a good person to ask.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Ah yes. To be honest, not much is known about them. The sect was destroyed long ago. However, it is said that this temple was once their home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of the temple, what happened here? The place isn't looking great.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: It was just before the plagues started! They burst up from under the cellar and trashed the place! It was late at night, so I couldn't see who they were, or if they were even human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Why wasn't the High Priest aware of this?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: The first of the plagues started that next morning. It didn't seem wise to bother him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure he'd have still liked to have been made aware. You say they came from beneath the cellar?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: That's right. Turns out there's quite the network of tunnels down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I think this is just what I'm after. I'd like to investigate these tunnels.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: I wouldn't if I were you. It's incredibly dangerous down there. We had a quick look, but there are some fearsome traps and vicious monsters lurking in the darkness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid it's essential that I go down there.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Well then you're either very brave or very foolish. We covered the tunnel they made with a trapdoor. Just head down into the cellar and then down that trapdoor.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Oh, it's also very dark down there. You should take some sort of light source and a tinderbox with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. You've been a great help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get down there.

sections.title: Talking to Jex again, before entering the cellar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jex.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: How's your investigation going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't made it down to the tunnels yet.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Well don't forget to take a light source and a tinderbox with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will. Thanks for the warning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Talking to High Priest, before entering the cellar

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck with getting our people out of Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Well just let me know if I can help in any way.

sections.title: The Sect of Scabaras
Talking to Sophanem Guard, before entering the tunnels

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: We're here to guard this place. Until recently, this was a bank, but then something came from the tunnels below and trashed the place.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: Now we're guarding the trapdoor down to the tunnels instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a tinderbox?

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: I do, but I'd like to hold on to it for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay, I have one with me anyway.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: I have one I could sell you for 50 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, that's too expensive.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: No problem. Come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a torch?

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: I do, but I'd like to hold on to it for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay, I have one with me anyway.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: I'll sell you an unlit one for 200 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, that's too expensive.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: No problem. Come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you're not spotty?

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: Ah, the pox. We weren't here when the outbreak took place. We were out of town trying to catch an antiques thief called Simon Templeton. We've only just got back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you afraid you'll fall ill as well?

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: The only reason we've been let back into the city is because the High Priest believes the pox isn't contagious.

lines.speaker: Sophanem Guard
lines.text: Besides, Sophanem is my home. It will take a lot more than the threat of a few spots to keep me away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't. I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go.

sections.title: Talking to Sophanem Guard when entering the tunnels

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: Be careful down there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will, I will.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: It really is incredibly dangerous down there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So people keep telling me.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: You should take some food down with you in case you get hurt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: I wouldn't take many valuables down there, just in case something bad happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have some faith in me.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: Watch out for the traps!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will, I will.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: If you see a scarab on any of the walls, stay well away from it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, why?

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: I saw one of the other guards disappear down a trapdoor when he approached it. We never saw him again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll look out for them then.

lines.speaker: Bank Guard
lines.text: Good luck!

sections.title: Talking to Jex, after entering the tunnels

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jex.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: How's your investigation going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad. Hopefully I'll make some real progress soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck, and be careful in those tunnels.

sections.title: Kaleef's corpse
Talking to Maisa

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What the... You're not Kaleef!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not, I'm [player name]. As for Kaleef, I'm afraid I have some bad news about him.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but Kaleef is dead.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Dead? He can't be dead! You're lying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's true. It looks like some creature killed him as he was entering this cavern.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Kaleef... Curse you, Osman!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were the two of you close?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What... I...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: No. I barely knew him. He was meant to relieve me of my posting up in Menaphos, but now I'm stuck.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Not that it matters I suppose. Even if he had made it, he wouldn't have been able to get across this chasm. We'd hoped there would be a route between the two cities down here, but clearly not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, that's kind of what I was hoping as well. I came down here to try and find a way into Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well good luck with that. If there was a way into the city, I'd have found it by now. This was my last hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have no way to leave?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Oh I could leave. While I may not know of any way into the city, I do know of a way out. Problem is, I was ordered not to leave until I found a way for Kaleef to get in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... That gives me an idea.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: An idea? What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The reason I'm looking for a way into Menaphos is so that I can help the citizens of Sophanem trapped there return home. However, maybe I don't actually need to enter the city to help them.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you knew of a way out of the city. You could help the citizens escape.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: And why would I do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because if you help me, I might be able to help you. You mentioned Osman, right? I'm guessing you work for him, which makes you a spy from Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: That's right... I'm Maisa. You know Osman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I'm the one who helped him free Prince Ali. I'm sure I could convince him to reconsider your posting.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hmm... If you are who you say you are, tell me where the Prince was being kept.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblin Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Then we have nothing to discuss.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Interesting. And who helped you free the Prince?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hassan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Louie Legs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leela.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ranael.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Then we have nothing to discuss.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You're obviously an imposter. You answered both questions incorrectly!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You'll have to do better than that to convince me.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Fair enough. I'm sorry I doubted you. I will help the citizens of Sophanem for you, but first you must convince Osman to come here and meet with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want him to come here?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Yes. No more secret messages or intermediaries. I want to speak to him, face to face.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll go to Al Kharid and see what I can do.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa again, after incorrectly guessing Prince Ali's prison location

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You again. If you are who you say you are, tell me where Prince Ali was being kept.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa again, after correctly answering but before speaking to Osman

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Have you brought Osman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Don't take too long. It's not safe for me to stay down here.

sections.title: I Spy with my Little Eye
Talking to Osman at Al Kharid

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to talk to you about Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Sophanem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Your help is needed urgently there.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: My help is needed all over the place. What makes you suggest that I'm more urgently needed in Sophanem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maisa wants to speak with you.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Maisa? You are mistaken. She's in Menaphos, and that city is locked tight to everyone, thanks in no small part to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? What have you been up to there?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Nothing you need to concern yourself with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't need to be so secretive with me. Did I not prove myself by freeing Prince Ali?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hmm... Very well. What do you know of the political landscape here in the Kharidian Desert?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a lot.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Well unlike many other regions, the Kharidian Desert is not united under a single banner. Instead, there are multiple independent city states, along with various lawless collectives.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: While all of the city states are currently at peace with each other, between some you'll find certain... rivalries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see. So one of those rivalries is between Menaphos and Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Some would say so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Including you?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: We were at war with the Menaphites for a long time. As Spymaster for Al Kharid, I know that no war is ever truly over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the Emir agree with you on that?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The Emir trusts me to protect the best interests of Al Kharid. So in answer to your original question, that's what I've been doing in Menaphos and Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, this is all very interesting, but one of your people, Kaleef, is dead. He was meant to relieve Maisa of her post in Menaphos, but now he can't.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Then I will send someone else down instead. Is that all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. Maisa is insisting that you meet with her personally.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That's not possible and she knows it. It would be tantamount to sticking one's head in a crocodile's mouth. If the Menaphites caught me, I'd be lynched on sight.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Now, return to her and tell her that I will send a replacement for Kaleef shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not going to work. There's a chasm blocking the way from Sophanem to Menaphos. Any agent you send won't be able to enter the city. That's why Maisa wants to speak with you.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: If there's no way for someone to relieve Maisa, then she will have to remain there herself. This is the job she signed up for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need Maisa to help me with something. She'll only do that if you go and speak to her.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That is your problem, not mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Prince Ali was your problem, but I still helped you.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: And you were paid for your services. What do you need from Maisa anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When Menaphos was locked down, many citizens from Sophanem were trapped there. I need to try and get them out of the city, but I have no way of entering Menaphos myself.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: So, you've convinced Maisa to help them on your behalf? What makes you think I want any part in this? The citizens of Sophanem are still Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Let's see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must be boring here.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You wish to charm me with the idea of getting my hands dirty? Well I'm perfectly happy here in Al Kharid. I did all the adventuring I ever wanted to do in my youth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There might be treasures underneath Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That there might be, but if so, those treasures belong to the dead. We have treasures aplenty here in Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It could drive a wedge between the Menaphite cities.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Menaphos wouldn't be happy if the citizens of Sophanem bypassed the quarantine. I imagine you'd be able to take advantage of that.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hmm... Alright, you have my curiosity. Let's see where this trail takes us. Meet me outside the gates to Sophanem. I have one or two things to sort out here before I depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would give you the chance to spy first hand.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I have Maisa and others in place to do that already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Perhaps because there is no suitable answer. Now, I must get on with my work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I give up.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: So you've seen sense? Good. Now, I must get on with my work.

sections.title: Talking to Osman at Al Kharid again, after failing to persuade him

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to talk to you about Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: As I've already told you, I have little interest in this business of yours. Why should I help you?

sections.title: Talking to Osman at Al Kharid again, after succeeding to persuade him

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to talk to you about Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Yes, yes. I have one or two things to sort here before I depart. I'll meet you outside the gates to Sophanem.

sections.title: Talking to Osman outside Sophanem

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There you are.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Ah, you're here. Do you know of a good way into the city? I'm getting too old for scaling walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong with using the gates?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Are you mad or stupid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Neither. Why?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: What if I'm recognised? Do you not think they'd question the presence of Al Kharid's Spymaster?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so maybe it wasn't one of my better suggestions.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Have any other bright ideas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know of a secret entrance to the north.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Perfect! Where is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a tunnel in some rocks near a camp.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Good. Now, wait for me here. There's no point in both of us risking our necks getting seen together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if you injure yourself down in the tunnels?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Don't worry about me. I may be getting older, but I didn't become Spymaster without getting into a fight or two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's...

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Give me two hours. If I'm not back by then, come and look for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

sections.title: Cut scene 2 - Osman sneaks into Sophanem

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Psst!

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osman's been gone for hours. I think I should have a look for him.

sections.title: Speaking to (not) Osman in Al Kharid

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to talk to you about Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I've no idea what's going on there. It's all on a need-to-know basis, and I don't need to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? Osman can't possibly be here!

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You can call me Osman, even though I am the man who is not Osman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... you're one of his agents then? But you look just like him?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Of course I look like him. I'm in disguise. What sort of spy would look like a spy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the last time I worked with Osman there was some business with really bad wigs.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Right... Let's change the subject to something Osman might talk about. I don't want to blow my cover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to go.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Until next time.

sections.title: Beetle battle
Talking to Jex

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jex.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Oh... hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: Some degenerate clubbed me and gave me a lump the size of an egg on the back of my head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no! I'll keep my eyes peeled for suspicious looking characters.

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: I have a fairly good idea who did it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Who?

lines.speaker: Jex
lines.text: That linen merchant, Siamun. He's never liked me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

sections.title: Cut scene 3 - Osman and Maisa speak below Sophanem

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: ... and whatever you do, don't risk giving yourself away when you help the citizens return.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I know what I'm doing Osman.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hang on... What's going on? What's that shaking?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Get out of here quick! Before the cave collapses!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osman!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh...

sections.title: Talking to Osman, after the battle

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I seem to recall telling you to wait for me. What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rescuing your ungrateful hide!

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I had everything under control.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well your two hours ran out a long time ago. Anyway, where did you disappear off to during the fight?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I didn't go anywhere. I decided to blend into the shadows. It's one of the many tricks I've picked up over the years. Not every problem needs a blade to solve it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So are you going to say 'thank you' or anything?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That's not required. Once again, I had it all under control.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Anyway, you'll be pleased to know that before we were interrupted, I was able to convince Maisa to remain in Menaphos. As agreed, she will help the citizens of Sophanem return home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear. I guess I should let the High Priest know.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Before you leave, it seems that Kaleef was able to at least get one hit on that beast. I suggest you take his dagger. It's quite powerful, and he has no need for it now.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Oh, and one more thing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Well done. You did an alright job here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I suppose.

sections.title: Contact established

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again. I have some wonderful news.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Menaphos just sent over some of their dead. Imagine our suprise when we found some of our own citizens disguised among them! Apparently we have both you and someone called Maisa to thank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Disguised among the dead? That's quite ingenious. I'm glad to hear they're home safe.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: It seems that we are once again in your debt. Please, take this as a thank you.
